G201: Logging `.exception(...)` should be used instead of `.error(..., exc_info=True)` (7 occurrences: lines 257, 385, 553, 564, 577, 649, 1031)

analyses.root_cause: A new file mooncake_d2rh_connector.py was added in PR #9648 without passing pre-commit lint checks. The file contains 24 ruff violations: 7 uses of `.error(exc_info=True)` that should be `.exception()`, 16 f-string logging violations, and 1 mutable default argument (B006). The ruff-check pre-commit hook failed, blocking the CI pipeline.
fix_suggestions: Replace all `.error(..., exc_info=True)` calls with `.exception(...)` (remove exc_info=True parameter)
Replace all f-string logging calls with lazy `%s` formatting, e.g. `logger.error(f'msg {var}')` -> `logger.error('msg %s', var)`
Fix line 896: change mutable default argument (e.g. `def func(arg={})`) to use `None` as default with an internal assignment pattern
Run `ruff check --fix vllm_ascend/distributed/kv_transfer/kv_p2p/mooncake_d2rh_connector.py` locally and verify with `pre-commit run ruff-check --all-files` before pushing
